# Nimbus Guidelines — Accessibility & Do's/Don'ts

> See [design.md](design.md) for token values. See [design-components.md](design-components.md) for component specs. See [design-preview.html](design-preview.html) for a live preview.

## Accessibility

### Contrast Requirements

| Requirement | Ratio |
|--------------|-------|
| Body text vs. background | 4.5:1 |
| Large text (≥ 24px or 19px bold) vs. background | 3:1 |
| UI component boundaries / icons vs. background | 3:1 |

| Component | 3:1 Against |
|-----------|--------------|
| Button outline | surface |
| Input border | surface |
| Focus ring | surface and primary |

### Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Move focus forward / backward |
| Enter / Space | Activate the focused button, link, or checkbox |
| Esc | Close the active modal, popover, or menu |
| Arrow keys | Move between items in a menu, tab list, or radio group |

### Assistive Technology

- Every icon-only button has an `aria-label` describing its action, not its appearance ("Delete task", not "Trash icon").
- Form fields use a real `<label for>` association — never rely on placeholder text alone as a label.
- Live status changes (task marked complete, comment posted) are announced via an `aria-live="polite"` region rather than relying on visual change alone.

## Pointer & Interaction

Nimbus is web-only, so this section replaces a gestures table.

- **Hover**: all interactive elements (buttons, rows, cards that are clickable) get a visible hover state within 100ms — no dead zones where hover changes nothing.
- **Focus rings**: always visible on keyboard focus (`:focus-visible`), never suppressed with `outline: none` without an equivalent replacement.
- **Cursor**: `pointer` on anything clickable, `text` on editable text, `grab`/`grabbing` on draggable task cards, `default` everywhere else.
- **Scroll**: the side nav rail and the task list scroll independently; the page itself never double-scrolls.

## Content Design

- Sentence case for all UI text — no Title Case, no ALL CAPS except single-letter badges (e.g. status dot labels).
- Button labels are 1–3 words, verb-first ("Create task", not "Task creation").
- Error messages state what went wrong and how to fix it in one sentence — never just "Something went wrong."
- Empty states name the action the user should take next, not just describe the absence of data.

## Do's and Don'ts

### Color
- **Do** reserve `primary` fill for the single most important action per view.
- **Don't** apply `primary` fill to more than one button in the same button group.

### Shape
- **Do** use `radius.md` consistently across cards, modals, and inputs so the page reads as one system.
- **Don't** mix a sharp-cornered card next to a rounded modal on the same screen.

### Elevation
- **Do** use elevation to indicate what's temporarily "on top" (dropdown, modal) — not as decoration on static content.
- **Don't** add a shadow to a card that isn't actually elevated above the page.

### Interaction
- **Do** show a pressed state within 1 frame of pointer-down.
- **Don't** rely on color alone to indicate a disabled control — pair it with reduced opacity and `cursor: not-allowed`.

### Layout
- **Do** collapse the side nav to a top bar below 720px.
- **Don't** truncate the primary nav item labels — use icons + full text or a drawer instead.

### Typography
- **Do** use Title for section headers within a page, Headline only for the page-level title.
- **Don't** use Display style outside the dashboard's top-level summary numbers.

### Motion
- **Do** use the short duration (120ms) for hover/press feedback so the UI feels responsive.
- **Don't** animate a modal open/close with anything longer than 320ms — it reads as sluggish.

### Components
- **Do** pair every destructive action (delete task, remove member) with a confirmation step.
- **Don't** introduce a new button variant for a one-off case — extend the existing Button spec instead.
